Provide comments/approval on the report or complete Appendix B
Document any actions taken from the review
Consider conducting full annual business review (optional, with Nedim's help)

threads.subject: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - Sql Server License Verification

llm_summary.one_line: Microsoft SQL Server license violation by client; clarify Aptum's liability and ownership.
llm_summary.summary: A client appears to be violating Microsoft SQL Server licensing terms. Barbara believes Aptum could be liable and fined in a Microsoft audit. Adam looped in Fred Taylor-Young (InfoSec/Abuse), who clarified that license policing shouldn't fall under Abuse and questions whether Aptum should police customer license compliance at all, given potential liability from misinterpretation. The ticket fell through cracks after the Abuse manager departed. Adam needs to decide: who owns this issue, what Aptum's liability exposure is, and what action to take with the client.
my_actions: Determine whether Legal or Compliance should assess Aptum's liability exposure for customer license violations
Clarify internal ownership: who handles customer software license compliance issues going forward
Decide on immediate action for this specific client situation (escalate, document, require remediation, etc.)
tracked_actions: Fred Taylor-Young and InfoSec documenting historical Abuse responsibilities and scope
llm_summary.suggested_reply: Thanks Fred. You raise valid questions about liability. I'm going to loop in Legal/Compliance to assess our exposure when customers violate third-party licenses on our infrastructure. In the meantime, let's document this client situation and ensure they're aware of the compliance risk. Barbara, can you confirm the client has been formally notified? We need a clear policy on this going forward.
